title: oxera subverticillata
intro: Oxera subverticillata, also known as New Caledonian oxeye, is a small shrub native to the Pacific island of New Caledonia. Its leaves and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Oxera subverticillata is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and potentially aiding in the treatment of certain digestive issues. The plant's leaves and flowers can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile herbal remedy in traditional medicine.
